heading: Community forest and open space conservation program
label: 2103d Community forest and open space conservation program
notes: Codificationand enacted identical sections. was repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

Findings, , , and , title VIII, § 8003(a), , , 2043, provided that:Pub. L. 110–234, title VIII, § 8003(a)122 Stat. 1281Pub. L. 110–246, § 4(a)122 Stat. 1664May 22, 2008June 18, 2008“Congress finds that—“(1)the Forest Service projects that, by calendar year 2030, approximately 44,000,000 acres of privately-owned forest land will be developed throughout the United States;“(2)public access to parcels of privately-owned forest land for outdoor recreational activities, including hunting, fishing, and trapping, has declined and, as a result, participation in those activities has also declined in cases in which public access is not secured;“(3)rising rates of obesity and other public health problems relating to the inactivity of the citizens of the United States have been shown to be ameliorated by improving public access to safe and attractive areas for outdoor recreation;“(4)in rapidly-growing communities of all sizes throughout the United States, remaining parcels of forest land play an essential role in protecting public water supplies;“(5)forest parcels owned by local governmental entities and nonprofit organizations are providing important demonstration sites for private landowners to learn forest management techniques;“(6)throughout the United States, communities of diverse types and sizes are deriving significant financial and community benefits from managing forest land owned by local governmental entities for timber and other forest products; and“(7)there is an urgent need for local governmental entities to be able to leverage financial resources in order to purchase important parcels of privately-owned forest land as the parcels are offered for sale.”[ and enacted identical provisions. was repealed by , set out as a note under , Agriculture.]Pub. children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 1 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a local governmental entity, Indian tribe, or nonprofit organization that owns or acquires a parcel under the program.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 2 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Local governmental entity
children.label: 3 Local governmental entity
children.text: The term “local governmental entity” includes any municipal government, county government, or other local government body with jurisdiction over local land use decisions.

children.label: A
children.text: is described in ; andsection 170(h)(3) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: operates in accordance with 1 or more of the purposes specified in .section 170(h)(4)(A) of title 26

children.heading: Program
children.label: 5 Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the community forest and open space conservation program established under subsection (b).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program, to be known as the “community forest and open space conservation program”.

children.label: A
children.text: are threatened by conversion to nonforest uses; and

children.label: i
children.text: economic benefits through sustainable forest management;

children.label: ii
children.text: environmental benefits, including clean water and wildlife habitat;

children.label: iii
children.text: benefits from forest-based educational programs, including vocational education programs in forestry;

children.label: iv
children.text: benefits from serving as models of effective forest stewardship for private landowners; and

children.label: v
children.text: recreational benefits, including hunting and fishing.

children.heading: Federal cost share
children.label: 2 Federal cost share
children.text: An eligible entity may receive a grant under the Program in an amount equal to not more than 50 percent of the cost of acquiring 1 or more parcels, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: 3 Non-Federal share
children.text: As a condition of receipt of the grant, an eligible entity that receives a grant under the Program shall provide, in cash, donation, or in kind, a non-Federal matching share in an amount that is at least equal to the amount of the grant received.

children.heading: Appraisal of parcels
children.label: 4 Appraisal of parcels
children.text: To determine the non-Federal share of the cost of a parcel of privately-owned forest land under paragraph (2), an eligible entity shall require appraisals of the land that comply with the Uniform Appraisal Standards for Federal Land Acquisitions developed by the Interagency Land Acquisition Conference.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the land to be acquired;

children.label: i
children.text: a description of community benefits to be achieved from the acquisition of the private forest land; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an explanation of the manner in which any private forest land to be acquired using funds from the grant will be managed; and

children.label: C
children.text: such other relevant information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Ineligibility
children.label: A Ineligibility
children.text: The Secretary shall not provide a grant under the Program for any project on land held in trust by the United States (including Indian reservations and allotment land).

children.heading: Acquired land
children.label: B Acquired land
children.text: No land acquired using a grant provided under the Program shall be converted to land held in trust by the United States on behalf of any Indian tribe.

children.heading: Applications to Secretary
children.label: 7 Applications to Secretary
children.text: The State forester or equivalent official (or in the case of an Indian tribe, an equivalent official of the Indian tribe) shall submit to the Secretary a list that includes a description of each project submitted by an eligible entity at such times and in such form as the Secretary shall prescribe.

children.heading: Duties of eligible entity
children.label: d Duties of eligible entity
children.text: An eligible entity shall provide public access to, and manage, forest land acquired with a grant under this section in a manner that is consistent with the purposes for which the land was acquired under the Program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3), an eligible entity that acquires a parcel under the Program shall not sell the parcel or convert the parcel to nonforest use.

children.heading: Reimbursement of funds
children.label: 2 Reimbursement of funds
children.text: An eligible entity that sells or converts to nonforest use a parcel acquired under the Program shall pay to the Federal Government an amount equal to the greater of the current sale price, or current appraised value, of the parcel.

children.heading: Loss of eligibility
children.label: 3 Loss of eligibility
children.text: An eligible entity that sells or converts a parcel acquired under the Program shall not be eligible for additional grants under the Program.

children.heading: State administration and technical assistance
children.label: f State administration and technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary may allocate not more than 10 percent of all funds made available to carry out the Program for each fiscal year to State foresters or equivalent officials (including equivalent officials of Indian tribes) for Program administration and technical assistance.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.
